Step 5: The Grand Offer, Part 4
ALL CONTENT © COPYRIGHT 2011 AND BEYOND | JON BENSON 3X
FORMULAS, INC. | ALL RIGHTS RESERVED | NO DUPLICATION ALLOWED
Jon: 	Welcome back to the tutorials, and let’s get into part 4 of the grand
offer, which is the pain/pleasure principle.
This is short. Nice change of pace from the other ones. This is where
you’re going to remind your viewer. At this point, this is where I talk
about twisting the knife. And I don’t mean to sound so morbid here
but, basically, at this point, you’ve got 90% of the people are ready to
buy.
In a webinar, by the way, this is very different. So, you may want to
look at my tutorials on the webinar, to get more specifics on that, on
why this is so completely different.
But what you want to make sure that you’re doing here is reminding
the user of the pain and future-pacing their pleasure. You want to tie
this to the moment of purchase. I’ll show you how.
Then, there’s the “not your fault, not your responsibility.” We’ve talked
about this in another video, but here’s where you bring it home again.
And then, there’s the reinforce of the guarantee. Here, you just want to
do the days and terms only. It’s real simple.
And optional, if you have them, is more testimonials. These are
testimonials, if you have them, from experts, testimonials based on
your service, testimonials based on your ethics. If you don’t have
those, you can just use normal testimonials. And if you don’t have
testimonials, that’s fine too. This is optional. And then, you’re going to
have the third 3X CTA.
So, let’s get into it. Let’s get into the specifics here.

-- 1 of 7 --

2
Here’s where you want to emphasize the pain of inaction, alongside
the pleasure of action. And here, the pleasure is future-paced. In
other words, what’s going to happen the moment you get something.
So, here’s the example slides of this. I start, again, with the word
“remember.” In the other videos, we talked about what “remember”
does to the subconscious. You’ve been struggling with your weight
loss for far too long. So, I am reminding them of what? The pain. The
pain of not having the solution to this particular problem.
If you have a service, you’re going to remind them of the pain of not
getting that service met. And if you have a brick-and-mortar business,
the pain of not getting the service to the quality that you deserve.
So, you’re reminding them of the pain. And I go on, “And the pain of
continuing in a state of inaction, will only lead to more despair, more
frustration, and continued weight gain.”
Notice how I’m tying the pain to inaction. Not taking action equals
more pain. But it’s specific pain. Let’s look at it again. The pain of
continuing in the state of inaction will only lead you to more despair,
more frustration, and continued weight gain.
Notice what I did first. I set up 2 very generic pain states, like despair,
sadness. Another part of it might be frustration and anger. It might be
something completely different.
You already know your anchors, you already know your pain states,
because you’ve done the homework before. And then, I finished with
the #1 desire of my viewer watching this video sales letter. In this
case, it’s to lose weight, so I say, “And continued weight gain. This is
what’s going to happen if you don’t take action.”
Then, we go into the future-pacing pleasure. “Yet, all of this can
change…” – notice the word there, “can change,” very good for weight
loss – “the moment you begin applying the quick start principles in the
Every Other Day Diet System.”
Here, I picked out a specific. I could have said, “Once you begin
applying the principles in the Every Other Day Diet System.” But I
decided to say, “the quick start principles.” Why? Because look what
your brain is going to pick up on here.
By the way, I could have emphasized the word “quick” here, and it
would have been clever. Looking back on it, I probably will.

-- 2 of 7 --

3
We’ve got “all this can change, quick.” Pretty cool, huh? The power of
subliminal highlighting. That’s not highlighted on the screen now, but
I’m going to go ahead and make that change.
But the emphasis is still there. The quick start principle is what’s going
to help them change.
So, here I say, “Picture the moment you finally have the answer you’ve
been looking for, and begin the path to the ideal you.”
“Picture.” What does that do? That sets up a dream story. That sets
up the imagination state. That’s where the mind clicks out of decision
mode and goes into future-pacing mode.
“Picture the answer” is highlighted.
This section of slides, by the way, has some gold in them, when it
comes down to what you should highlight.
So, “Picture the moment you finally have the answer you’ve been
looking for. You feel alive.” Notice I don’t say, “You will feel alive,” or
“you’re about to feel alive,” not that those are horrible ways to say that.
I’m future-pacing 100%, at this point. In other words, “Picture the
moment you have the answer. You feel alive, full of hope and
optimism. You see the weight coming off in the bathroom mirror today,
and every morning after.”
Now, notice the wording here. “You see...” I’m looking, again, at what
see, senses, and all that. “The weight coming off in the bathroom
mirror…,” so I’m giving them something tangible to look toward.
“Today.”
Now, when you’re reading this, your conscious mind is thinking, “Wow,
I can lose weight today?” Or, for another product, “Wow, I could make
money today?” “Wow, I could book this nice hotel today?” No, that’s
not what you’re saying. You’re future-pacing them. In other words, the
moment you get this answer, picture what your life will be like.
So, today is a day in the future. It’s just not worded that way. And
then I say, “And every morning after. You can see the weight coming
off in the bathroom, today. Every morning after. You are experiencing
the energy, vitality, and the life-renewing results you deserve, at long
last.”
Again, notice the wording here. “You are experiencing.” Not “you will
be experiencing,” “you are experiencing the energy, the vitality, and the
life-rewarding results you deserve, at long last.”

-- 3 of 7 --

4
Now, that walks you right through the pain/pleasure aspect of that, the
very first part of that. And now comes the reminder – the reminder
that’s so important, that the pain is not their fault but the action is their
responsibility. We talked about this in an earlier video, like I said,
where we talked about the words “it’s not your fault” are the 4 most
powerful words you can use.
The 4 most powerful words you can use: “It’s not your fault. But it is
your responsibility now, because you know the answers.” This is how
we build this.
“Yes, it’s true.” Great power phrase, right off the bat. “Your weight
gain, until today, has largely been no fault of your own. It’s true that
you’ve been lied to by all those quick pill solutions, the fad Hollywood
diets.” Notice what I’m doing here. I’m repeating the same lines I’ve
already told them that they’ve been buying into. “And the false
promises no one stands behind.”
“However, today marks your day to take responsibility, to take the
action necessary to put an end to the lies, and begin on a true,
lifestyle-friendly weight loss plan that simply works.”
Notice the slide here. It’s very important. “Take the action necessary.”
Let’s go back, actually, to the first one.
Let’s take responsibility. Responsibility can be an, “Ew, I don’t want to
take responsibility” kind of reaction. So, here’s what makes this
powerful. I say, “You’ve been lied to. It’s true. It’s largely not your
fault. But today marks your day to take responsibility. Today, now you
know the answers, because you’re going to put an end to the lies.”
So, I don’t just say, “You’re going to start losing weight,” “you’re going
to end the lies.”
Now, everyone listening to this can take that and apply this. “End the
lies, end the misinformation, end the confusion,” you can extend this as
far as you want to go, “and begin anew on a true,” fill-in-the-blank.
Whatever your benefit is. In my case, lifestyle-friendly weight loss
plan. Where does that come from? It comes from my what?
Everyone say it out loud. It comes from my USP.
Of course, I wouldn’t hear you if you did say it out loud. Right?
But end with a massive anchor drop, “that simply works.” Simply
works. Powerful words. “Simply works” implies simplicity.

-- 4 of 7 --

5
So, next, you’re going to need to reinforce the guarantee again.
You’ve covered it once, but this is just a general reinforcement that
assures them, along the way, “Don’t forget, this is a guarantee.” So,
this is how I word it. It’s very simple.
“And unlike those phony solutions,” so I tie it right back to the pain,
because we’re still in the pain/pleasure mode. “Unlike those phony
solutions, this one’s guaranteed to work.”
Notice what I did here. Pain/pleasure. Before, I did it on slides that
were following each other. I said, “Oh, this pain, this pain, this pain!
However, pleasure, pleasure, pleasure.” And now, I can take
advantage of that same technique, except do it on the same slide.
Unlike pain, this one pleasure. It’s the same technique. Just put it into
one slide.
So, I show them the guarantee, again. I show them my proof seals.
And I say, “Unlike those phony solutions (pain), this one is guaranteed
to work (pleasure).
And then, I repeat the guarantee. “Try it for 60 full days, and see the
results you desire, or you pay absolutely nothing.”
I don’t go back into the triple guarantee here. You can, but I’m trying to
save a little bit of slide time, and you don’t need to do it. They already
know about the triple guarantee, and you just say, “Try it for 60 days,
pay absolutely nothing.”
Now, I include something else here. This is a cool little inclusion.
“Plus,” anchor drop, “you’re always minutes away from an answer from
our friendly support staff.” Notice that I have not brought this up, yet.
You want to include something about service, support, ethics, those
kinds of things we talked about earlier, in this section of your VSL.
This is a great way to do it, because notice where the friendly support
staff is worded – on a slide that has a guarantee seal on it, with all
those security, this, that, the other. Pretty cool.
So, support is really over-emphasized.
You can now include more testimonials, if you have them. Again, this
is optional. This is where I like to include professional expert
testimonials. This works really well. And if you don’t have those, you
can use testimonials reflecting on your service, the quality of your
service, the ease of purchase, the ease of taking advantage of your
service, those kinds of things.

-- 5 of 7 --

6
So, it’s not just about, “Well, I had this great time,” or “Wow, I lost all
this weight,” or “Wow, I made all this money.” It could be, “Purchasing
this product was so easy! It was exactly as Jon described. It was just
so quick, and I had no hassles.
You know, I didn’t get my files immediately, so I contacted customer
service. And within an hour, I had everything I needed. Awesome
customer service!”
Those are great testimonials to have, at this point. What I opted to do
was professional testimonials, because I have some damn good ones.
So, here are some example slides. And here’s how I position it.
“You’ve heard from everyday men and women, folks just like you,”
again, inclusion, “who have seen incredible success with the EODD
System. Now, let’s hear from the experts.”
The experts? Who are the experts? So, I go into the doctors. And I
say, “John Matt Yuill is an MD, board-certified, internal medicine, and a
client. And he says we’ve done an excellent job of providing sound
nutritional advice, strong motivation.
Dr. Howard Lucille says, “It combines science with the doability to
deliver outstanding results for everyone. I’m basically going to read
this.
I say, “Even hardcore diet skeptic,” and John is, by the way, “Dr. John
Berardi, he’s an assistant adjunct professor at the University of Texas,
but he’s also the president of Precision Nutrition, and he works with
high-level professional athletes.
So, he says, “As both a scientist and a coach, I don’t go for gimmicks.
So, when evaluating the Every Other Day Diet, I asked myself 3
questions. Is this scientifically sound? Does it have real-world
support? And is it a program that people will actually follow? And the
answer to all 3 is yes.”
So, those are 3 very powerful, very strong, professional testimonials.
Now, you conclude part 4 with another 3X CTA. But don’t worry,
you’ve already written this. This can be exact repeats of previous
slides. It’s going to look like this. “Look below this video now. Click on
the orange button that says ‘Add To Cart’ to claim your discounted
price now. You’ll be granted immediate access to everything,” I
include the immediate access slide, “the Every Other Day Diet System,
and all the bonuses, plus your free virtual trainer bonus worth $97, etc.
Take immediate action today.”
